# tokens: # Compare-dispatch spec — covers the three layers wired through
#   1. CORE comparators on the kernel scalar types (Integer, Decimal,
#      Number cross-promotion, String, Boolean, Atom). Attached to
#   2. NATIVE comparators on lang-layer domain types (Date, DateTime,
#      Instant, TimeOfDay). Attached to the existing FormatBehavior
#   3. USER-DEFINED comparators installed by the `cmp` word
#      lets the caller write a bare type-name Word — the /q at the
#      sig position captures it as an Atom; the handler resolves to
#      the registered *Type and wraps its Behavior with a body that
# Integer ordering — the numberCompareBehavior on TNumber handles
# both Integer-Integer pairs (via the lattice walk) and Decimal pairs.
# Decimal ordering — same dispatch path, AsNumber coerces.
# Cross-type Integer + Decimal: LCA = Number; both values flow
# through AsNumber.  Without the LCA walk the dispatch would need
# a per-pair Behavior on Integer or Decimal — instead a single
# Comparer on Number handles the whole numeric branch.
# Cross-branch (Integer vs String) — no shared Comparer below the
# root, so the dispatch surfaces an error rather than silently
# 2. NATIVE: predefined domain comparators (Date / DateTime / Instant)
# These types are constructed via the `aql:time` native module — the
# test runner pre-installs its exports so spec rows can use
# Two Instants from different unix timestamps order numerically.
# ClkDuration ordering: built from numeric helpers.
# `cmp Person [body]` — Person is a Word, captured as an Atom by the
# sig's /q at position 0. The handler resolves "Person" to its *Type
# and wraps the Behavior with a Comparer running the body. Inside the
# body, `a` and `b` are pushed as defs; the trailing Integer on the
# Ascending by age: body subtracts (b.age - a.age sub computes b-a).
# `make` calls are parenthesised so `def NAME (make TYPE BODY)`
# binds NAME to the resulting instance — without the parens the
# trailing tokens get pulled into the next forward collection.
type Person object {age:Integer} cmp Person [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub] def alice (make Person {age:30}) def bob (make Person {age:25}) alice bob lt
type Person object {age:Integer} cmp Person [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub] def alice (make Person {age:30}) def bob (make Person {age:25}) bob alice lt
type Person object {age:Integer} cmp Person [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub] def alice (make Person {age:30}) def bob (make Person {age:25}) alice bob gt
# Descending: swap operands in the body — flips the ordering.
type Item object {n:Integer} cmp Item [(b 'n' get) (a 'n' get) sub] def i1 (make Item {n:1}) def i9 (make Item {n:9}) i1 i9 lt
type Item object {n:Integer} cmp Item [(b 'n' get) (a 'n' get) sub] def i1 (make Item {n:1}) def i9 (make Item {n:9}) i9 i1 lt
# Equality through cmp: a == a returns 0, lt/gt false, lte/gte true.
type Person object {age:Integer} cmp Person [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub] def alice (make Person {age:30}) alice alice lt
type Person object {age:Integer} cmp Person [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub] def alice (make Person {age:30}) alice alice lte
# Unknown type — surfaces a clear error.
# Body produced no Integer — error propagates.
type Faulty object {x:Integer} cmp Faulty [a b quux] def v1 (make Faulty {x:1}) def v2 (make Faulty {x:2}) v1 v2 lt
